title: Tether (USDT)
description: The world's most widely used stablecoin, pegged 1:1 to the US dollar. Tether provides liquidity and stability across multiple blockchain networks including Ethereum, Bitcoin, and Solana.

title: USD Coin (USDC)
description: A fully collateralized US dollar stablecoin created by Circle and Coinbase. USDC is regulated, audited, and backed 1:1 by US dollar reserves and short-term US treasuries.

title: Ethena USDe (USDe)
description: A delta-neutral stablecoin that maintains a $1 peg through a combination of collateral and derivatives. USDe offers stability without traditional banking infrastructure dependencies.

title: Dai (DAI)
description: A decentralized stablecoin generated by the MakerDAO protocol. DAI is backed by collateralized cryptocurrency and maintains its $1 peg through incentive mechanisms and governance.

title: MakerDAO (MKR)
description: The governance token of the MakerDAO protocol. MKR holders vote on protocol parameters and stability fees. It's essential for the creation and management of the DAI stablecoin ecosystem.

title: PayPal USD (PYUSD)
description: A stablecoin issued by PayPal and supported by PayPal's balance sheet. PYUSD combines blockchain technology with PayPal's trust and regulatory compliance for seamless digital payments.

title: Global Dollar (USDG)
description: An emerging stablecoin designed for global financial inclusion. USDG aims to provide a stable medium of exchange with enhanced accessibility for international transactions and cross-border payments.
